meaning: adj.幽灵般的,怪异的,神经质的
examples: From bizarre antimatter to experiments that tie light up in knots, physics has revealed some spooky sides of our world.
I was just thinking about her when she phoned. Spooky!
He started telling everybody in the bar about the horrible experience he just went through in the spooky car.
What it does is so soothing it's actually spooky, bringing to mind Arthur C.
Meanwhile, as I wrote in early April, the similarities to a year ago are spooky.
